title: Spectral Estimation
description: Why a single raw FFT is a poor PSD estimator: bias/variance tradeoffs, windowing and spectral leakage, and Bartlett/Welch periodogram averaging.
objectives: Explain why the raw periodogram is a biased, inconsistent estimator of the true power spectral density
Describe the bias-variance tradeoff that windowing and segment-averaging navigate
Explain how Bartlett's and Welch's methods average segment periodograms to reduce variance
